channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Man Is Charged With Providing Gun to Louisiana Shooter
item.description: The arrest came two days after a gunman carried out a rampage that left eight children dead in Shreveport and injured two adults.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales
item.media:description: A vigil mourned eight children fatally shot in Shreveport, La.

item.title: Virginia Passes Gerrymandered Map to Help Democrats in Midterms: 4 Takeaways
item.description: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: National party leaders were heavily invested in Virginia’s referendum, and tens of millions of dollars flowed in.

item.title: Immigration Agent Is Charged With Assaulting a Colorado Protester
item.description: The officer faces a misdemeanor charge after an encounter in Colorado, becoming one of the few federal agents to be prosecuted by local officials during the immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Nina Riggio for The New York Times
item.media:description: Franci Stagi, the protester the officer is seen grabbing on video last year in Colorado, was happy to see charges but felt they were too lenient.

item.title: Here’s What the New Virginia House Map Looks Like
item.description: Democrats now hold six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the new map could allow the party to win 10 of them.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Here’s Where the National Fight Over Gerrymandered Maps Stands
item.description: With Virginia’s vote on Tuesday, Democrats pulled close to even with Republicans, who may have further cards to play in the race to gain extra House seats.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: What Was the 2015 Iran Nuclear Deal?
item.description: President Trump has ridiculed President Barack Obama and the agreement, which he withdrew from during his first term. His critics say he could have avoided a war had he left it in place.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Negotiators from the United States, Iran and European countries meeting to discuss a nuclear deal in Vienna in 2015.

item.title: Virginia Passes New House Map in a Midterm Victory for Democrats
item.description: The map, as extreme a gerrymander as exists in the United States, could let Democrats win 10 of the state’s 11 House seats as the party tries to win back the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: A voter casting their ballot during early voting in Burke, Va.

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Texas Ten Commandments Law
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit said the law does not violate the Constitution. The plaintiffs said they planned to ask the Supreme Court to reverse the decision.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Ten Commandments poster in an English classroom at Fossil Ridge High School in Fort Worth.

item.title: Anger Evident in Red Areas as Virginians Cast Their Votes
item.description: “It’s just unfair for the Democrats to have 10 seats and the Republicans to have one,” one voter said of the proposed House map. But others in the state’s blue northern areas were more supportive.
item.dc:creator: Nan K. Chase, Leah Small and Darren Sands
item.media:description: Members of the Madison County Republican Committee demonstrated in opposition of the Virginia redistricting referendum during early voting earlier this month in Madison, Va.

item.title: The New Virginia Map Could Add Four Democratic Seats
item.description: The party now holds six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the proposed map could allow Democrats to win 10 of them.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Dark Money Dominates Fund-Raising in the Virginia Referendum Vote
item.description: Of the $98 million raised by the three groups that have bought the most advertising, at least 96 percent has come from nonprofit groups that do not disclose their donors.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Matt Zdun
item.media:description: Both proponents and opponents of the Virginia redistricting referendum have depended to an extraordinary degree on money raised from nonprofit groups that do not disclose their donors.

item.title: Serial Rapist Pleads Guilty to 2 Murders After Chewed Gum Links DNA to Crimes
item.description: Mitchell A. Gaff, 68, acknowledged killing two women who were sexually assaulted before being found dead in their Washington State apartments in the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:description: A photo of Susan Vesey, who was killed in 1980.

item.title: Key Areas to Watch in Virginia’s Redistricting Election
item.description: Suburbs to the north and large cities to the south could offer insight into how support for the amendment to the state constitution is unfolding.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang and Alex Lemonides

item.title: Tucker Carlson Says He Is ‘Tormented’ by His Past Support for Trump
item.description: “I want to say I’m sorry for misleading people,” said the conservative commentator, who has broken sharply with the president over the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tucker Carlson, the former Fox News host, has feuded with President Trump and his allies for weeks over the war with Iran.

item.title: Polls in Virginia Redistricting Measure Show Close Margins and Few Undecided Voters
item.description: A redistricting measure is asking voters to temporarily reverse a decision they made less than six years ago.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:description: A voter leaving a poll site at Fairfax Government Center in Fairfax, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: Election Day Turnout for Virginia Redistricting Measure Shows Significant Decline
item.description: In-person turnout on Tuesday is trailing far behind that of last year’s governor’s race, which set a state record in a nonpresidential election.
item.dc:creator: Luke Vrotsos and Alex Lemonides
item.media:description: Voting in the Virginia redistricting referendum at a school in Alexandria, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: D.H.S. Will Run Out of Money for Paychecks in May, Secretary Says
item.description: The issue threatens to renew chaos at airports as lawmakers remain divided over a deal to end the two-month shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Michael Gold
item.media:description: A Transportation Security Administration officer at Pittsburgh International Airport in March, when some U.S. travelers faced long security lines at airports.

item.title: Judge Bars Imminent Removal of D.C. Bike Lanes
item.description: A federal judge found the National Park Service had improperly rushed plans to strip out bike lanes connecting downtown Washington to the Tidal Basin.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:description: Cyclists during a rally in support of protected bike lanes in Washington in February.

item.title: U.S. Abruptly Rescinds Subpoenas in John Brennan Inquiry
item.description: The reversal came after the Justice Department replaced a career prosecutor with a loyalist to President Trump in the administration’s effort to charge the former C.I.A. chief with a crime.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
item.media:description: John O. Brennan testifying to Congress in 2017.

item.title: RFK Jr. Refused to Commit to Backing New CDC Director on Vaccines
item.description: In a tense congressional hearing, the health secretary also said he bore no responsibility for the measles outbreak in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. pushed back on Democrats’ assertions that he was responsible for the country’s worst measles outbreak in decades.

item.title: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a Florida Democrat, Resigns From Congress
item.description: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick stands accused of stealing $5 million in federal funds. She said she would step down minutes before an ethics panel was to vote on whether to recommend expelling her.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:description: Representative Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick wrote on social media that the House Ethics Committee had refused to give her lawyers time to prepare her defense

item.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
item.description: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during peace talks with Iran in Islamabad earlier this month.

item.title: Ohio State Details Relationship that Led to Former President Walter Carter Jr.’s Resignation
item.description: The university released a report that said the president, Walter Carter Jr., took trips with a podcaster and suggested that the university hire her.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Saul
item.media:description: Walter Carter Jr. resigned from the Ohio State presidency after disclosing a relationship with podcaster.

item.title: Senate Republicans Push Forward on Measure to Fund ICE Through 2029
item.description: The resolution would pave the way for Republicans to push through a $70 billion infusion to pay for immigration enforcement for the remainder of President Trump’s tenure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham wrote the latest budget blueprint, part of Republicans’ plan to fund the Department of Homeland Security on two tracks.

item.title: Could the Next Football Hall of Famer Be … Teddy Roosevelt?
item.description: A cabinet secretary made the case for the hero of San Juan Hill to be inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame. Here’s why.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: Theodore Roosevelt in 1912, after he left the presidency.

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Cease-Fire Before Deadline Expires; Vance’s Trip Is on Hold
item.description: President Trump attributed his decision to divisions among the Iranians about how to proceed and said he wanted to give them more time.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Maggie Haberman and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad, Pakistan, this month during a previous round of negotiations between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Pentagon to Stop Requiring Members of Military to Get Flu Vaccines
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth called the mandate “overreaching” in announcing the policy change. The vaccine will remain available to service members who want to be inoculated.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday that service members would no longer have to get flu vaccines.

item.title: The Redistricting War Has Come to Virginia
item.description: Voters in Virginia are deciding whether to adopt a new gerrymandered congressional map that benefits Democrats. Nick Corasaniti, a politics reporter, explains the role Virginia’s new map is playing in the nationwide redistricting battle and its potential implications for the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Laura Bult, June Kim and James Surdam

item.title: Aftershocks from ‘The Shadow Papers’
item.description: Publication of a trove of confidential Supreme Court memos ignited debates in the legal academy.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak
item.category: New York Times v Sullivan (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:credit: Gabriella Demczuk for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Supreme Court in 2016.

item.title: Florida Inquiry Into ChatGPT’s Role in FSU Shooting Shifts to Criminal Investigation
item.description: The state opened a criminal investigation into the chatbot and its maker involving a fatal shooting last year. The company said it’s not responsible for the killings.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Erich Martin for The New York Times
item.media:description: Two adults died and six other people were injured last April at the shooting near the student union at Florida State.

item.title: Justice Dept. Charges Prominent Civil Rights Group With Financial Crimes
item.description: Republicans have accused the Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Southern Poverty Law Center said that it was being investigated by the Justice Department over its past use of paid informants to infiltrate extremist groups.

item.title: A Year After U.S.A.I.D.’s Death, Fired Workers Find Few Jobs and Much Loss
item.description: People have plowed through savings, cashed out retirement funds and moved in with relatives. Former U.S.A.I.D. workers estimate that less than half have found full-time work.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Bumiller and Eileen Sullivan
item.category: United States Agency for International Development
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: F.A.A. Investigates Close Call Between Southwest Planes in Nashville
item.description: An aborted landing at Nashville International Airport on Saturday put one of the planes on a potential collision course with another plane taking off.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.media:description: Southwest Airlines planes at Nashville International Airport last year.

item.title: ‘Donnyland’? Ukraine Proposes Naming Part of the Donbas in Trump’s Honor.
item.description: The proposal reflects a global reality in which governments appeal to President Trump’s vanity to get American might on their side.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Lynsey Addario for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sloviansk, in the industrial Donbas region, which was first invaded by Russia in 2014.

item.title: What Happens When A.I. Runs a Store in San Francisco?
item.description: Andon Market in San Francisco is billed as the first retail boutique run by an artificial intelligence agent. So far, the inventory seems random, and there are too many candles.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andon Market is billed as the world’s first retail boutique run by an artificial intelligence agent.

item.title: Supreme Court Appears to Back F.C.C. Fines Against AT&T and Verizon
item.description: AT&T and Verizon were penalized millions of dollars for what the agency said was a failure to protect consumer information. The companies say they were deprived of their right to a jury trial.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brendan Carr, the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission. In the case before the Supreme Court on Tuesday, the Trump administration is defending the agency’s use of fines, calling them one of the “most important and frequently used enforcement tools.”

item.title: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger Stressed Pragmatism, But Politics Hound Her
item.description: Gov. Abigail Spanberger of Virginia has emphasized practical governance as her brand, but the political fight over redistricting has dominated her term so far.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson
item.media:description: Gov. Abigail Spanberger of Virginia took office in January.

item.title: Virginia Redistricting Referendum: What to Watch
item.description: Republicans have built a small advantage from the gerrymandering clash so far, but Virginia voters could change that.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein and Nick Corasaniti

item.title: What to Know About Virginia’s Redistricting Fight
item.description: The election on Tuesday is part of a coast-to-coast fight between Republicans and Democrats ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Photographs by Ryan M. Kelly/Associated Press
item.media:description: State Senators Ryan T. McDougle and Scott A. Surovell, a Republican and a Democrat on opposing sides of the Virginia referendum.
